content_of_post_to_answer: How can we learn to see everything in life as just ordinary, so we don't overthink?
answer: Trying to see everything as ordinary usually fails when the mind is still treating events as evidence about who you are or what will happen next. Overthinking is often just meaning inflation: small things get promoted into symbols. The shift isn't dulling life but refusing to promote every thought into a story. Most things don't need interpretation, just passage.
